summary: Validated all 8 public download endpoints for the X-39MATRIX Sevilla pitch deck v4.1 (/api/pitch/*) and the Camara de Sevilla proposal letter (/api/camara/*). All 20 pytest assertions PASSED including HTTP status, content-type, file size ranges, magic bytes (%PDF for PDFs, OpenTimestamps header for .ots), SHA-256 consistency between pitch PDF bytes and the /api/pitch/v4_1.sha256 JSON endpoint, Content-Disposition filename, and presence of required Spanish keywords ('X-39MATRIX', 'Francisco Leal', 'TIC Negocios', '+34 643 105 983', '## 1. Que es X-39MATRIX', 'Capa 10') in HTML/TXT/MD bodies.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is ~608 lines, still within acceptable range but trending large; future refactor could split download endpoints into a routers/downloads.py module.
All download endpoints correctly use FileResponse with explicit media_type and filename, and 404 fallback when file missing. Solid implementation.
test_credentials: Not required - all tested endpoints are PUBLIC.
seed_data_creation: None. Existing static files in /app/backend/static were used as-is.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pitch/Camara download endpoints verified end-to-end via public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. SHA-256 of pitch PDF = 9bf4f6eacc2ada98e538352632ed3eae905604a29cef39771b14a07b4f123b4e (matches expected). Pitch PDF = 95952 bytes, Camara PDF = 57222 bytes, both .ots files = 630 bytes with valid OpenTimestamps header. Test file is reusable for regression: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_pitch_camara_downloads.py -v
rca of the issue: User's original 'No such file or directory' error was a local client-side issue (had not downloaded files locally). Server-side delivery confirmed fully functional: all 8 endpoints return correct binary payloads with valid magic bytes, correct sizes, and SHA-256 integrity.
